setting.note: Kamakura, the old seaside capital, where one sleepless night the aging Shingo Ogata hears a deep sound from the wooded hills behind his house and takes it for a summons. He commutes to a Tokyo office along the line north, so the map runs from the temple town to the gardens of the capital.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Yasunari Kawabata serialised the novel between 1949 and 1954; it first appeared as a book in 1954. Kawabata died in 1972, so the Japanese text is not yet public domain. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.translation: The standard English translation, by Edward Seidensticker (1970), is also in copyright, which is another reason this cannot be a sourced book.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text or any translation, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Sound of the Mountain, Yasunari Kawabata (1954). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The cypress green of the wooded hills that rise behind the Ogata house, where the sound of the mountain comes from. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper at 6.74:1.
